name: Bebo Valdés
abstract: Dionisio Ramón Emilio Valdés Amaro (October 9, 1918 – March 22, 2013), better known as Bebo Valdés, was a Cuban pianist, bandleader, composer and arranger. He was a central figure in the golden age of Cuban music, especially due to his big band arrangements and compositions of mambo, chachachá and batanga, a genre he created in 1952.

concerts.label: Fujitsu Jazz Festival: Tribute to Paquito D'Rivera
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: We were the . . . the rhthym section of Bebo Valdés’ Orquesta Ritmo de Batanga.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Paquito D'Rivera
about_quotes.evidence: We played Carnegie Hall in 1978, and it was a coincidence that Bebo Valdés was visiting New York when that was happening. So, he got the chance to see his son after almost 20 years without seeing him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paquito D'Rivera — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
